content.problemStatement:
You work as an engineer. You were given an empty board with KKK consecutive cells; at any moment, each cell can display one character.

You want the board to display a string SSS with length N>KN > KN>K. Since the board isn't large enough, you want to display the string in N‚àíK+1N-K+1N‚àíK+1 steps. In the iii-th step (1‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK+11 \le i \le N-K+11‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK+1), you'll make the board display the characters Si,Si+1,‚Ä¶,Si+K‚àí1S_i, S_{i+1}, \dots, S_{i+K-1}Si‚Äã,Si+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Si+K‚àí1‚Äã.

The power required to switch the board from step iii to step i+1i+1i+1 (for 1‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK1 \le i \le N-K1‚â§i‚â§N‚àíK) is equal to the number of characters displayed on the board that have to change between these steps. You should find the total power required for the whole process of displaying a string, i.e. the sum of powers required for switching between all consecutive pairs of steps.
constraints: each character of SSS is a lowercase English letter
the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains a single string SSS with length NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the total power required for text switching.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

In step 111, the board is displaying "aab".
In step 222, the board is displaying "abb".
In step 333, the board is displaying "bbc".
In step 444, the board is displaying "bcc".

The power required for switching from the 111-st to the 222-nd step is 111, because cell 111 changes from 'a' to 'a' (requiring power 000), cell 222 changes from 'a' to 'b' (requiring power 111) and cell 333 changes from 'b' to 'b' (requiring power 000); 0+1+0=10 + 1 + 0 = 10+1+0=1.
The power required for switching between the 222-nd and 333-rd step is 222 and the power required for switching between the 333-rd and 444-th step is 111.
Therefore, the answer is 1+2+1=41 + 2 + 1 = 41+2+1=4.
